page: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top 10%. Memory performance is also highly rated, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching top-tier levels, and large-block read and write bandwidth placing in the top 25%. However, single-threaded operations show more moderate performance, with gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average range.

The instance offers high resource density and compute efficiency for highly parallelized workloads, utilizing dedicated physical cores without simultaneous multithreading. This design maximizes per-core performance predictability but represents a tradeoff for workloads that benefit from hyperthreading. The absence of local storage means all data persistence must rely on network-attached storage, which is supported by the high-speed 100 Gbps network interface.

Given its architectural profile and benchmark strengths, the hpc6a.48xlarge is suited for high-performance computing applications, large-scale parallel simulations, software compilation, and physics calculations. It is also highly capable for memory-intensive database operations and Redis deployments, though it is less optimal for GPU-dependent machine learning training or large language model text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6a.48xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but delivers a high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and strong database operations, though single-threaded tasks perform in the average range. This hardware profile offers high resource density for parallelized compute workloads. It is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale simulations, software compilation, and complex physics modeling.

384 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
